title: List of Medical Devices for an Urgent Public Health Need in Relation to COVID-19 - Canada.ca
content:
## ARCHIVED: List of Medical Devices for an Urgent Public Health Need in Relation to COVID-19
This is an archived list. Please refer to the most current version of this list.
This List  of Medical Devices for an Urgent Public Health Need in Relation to COVID-19 (UPHN list) is incorporated by reference in the Medical Devices  Regulations (MDR) in the definition of UPHN medical device and  in section 68.01.
Pursuant to subsection 68.1(1) of the MDR , the Minister of  Health (the Minister) may add medical devices to Part 1 of the UPHN list.  The Minister has reasonable grounds to believe that there is an urgent public  health need (UPHN) related to COVID-19 for these devices.
Pursuant to subsection 68.1(2) of the MDR , the Minister may  add categories of medical devices to Part 2 of the UPHN list . The Minister has reasonable  grounds to believe that there is a UPHN related to COVID-19 for the devices  that belong to those categories.
As the pandemic evolves, Health Canada will continue to assess  the UPHN for the devices and categories of devices on the List.
For more information, please refer to:
- Medical devices for use in relation to COVID-19
Date issued: 2023-11-09 Supersedes: 2023-02-22
Access the archived version of this list.
Identifier of device
Category of medical devices
medical devices that do not belong to any other category of medical devices set out in Part 2 of the List for which an application for an authorization was submitted to the Minister under Interim Order No. 3 Footnote 1 before the day on which Part 1.1 of the MDR came into force, and in respect of that application: no decision had been made under the Interim Order before that day, no decision has been made under Part 1.1 of the Regulations, and the applicant has not withdrawn the application.
medical devices that do not belong to any other category of medical devices set out in Part 2 of the List for which an application for an authorization was submitted to the Minister under Interim Order No. 3 Footnote 1 before the day on which Part 1.1 of the MDR came into force, and in respect of that application:
- no decision had been made under the Interim Order before that day,
- no decision has been made under Part 1.1 of the Regulations, and
- the applicant has not withdrawn the application.
Footnote 1 Interim Order No. 3 Respecting the Importation and Sale of Medical Devices for Use in Relation to COVID-19 Return to footnote 1 referrer
